page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance type powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 32 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, delivering 32 vCPUs. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the processor delivers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and Geekbench multi-core score both rank in the middle 50% tier, whereas single-core metrics, including Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, fall into the bottom 25% category. Single-threaded gzip compression performance is poor, placing in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-threaded operations such as bzip3 decompression, software compilation with clang, and ray tracing achieve average tier results. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently register in the middle 50% range.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is the disparity between its multi-core and single-core performance. While the 32 dedicated cores provide sufficient parallel processing capacity for multi-threaded workloads, applications dependent on high single-thread throughput will experience performance limitations. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a standard resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads. It is appropriate for multi-threaded tasks such as static web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale LLM inference using small to medium models, where parallel execution offsets the weaker single-core speed.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 10 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff: while multi-core metrics, memory bandwidth, and database operations rank in the average tier, single-core performance is weak, with single-threaded gzip compression falling into the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for parallelized workloads such as multi-threaded web serving, database operations, software compilation, and medium-model LLM inference, where multi-core execution can mitigate the lower single-thread efficiency.
